overview.name: Paper Notebook
overview.description: A bound collection of paper sheets used for writing, note-taking, journaling, and drawing. Available in various formats including spiral-bound, hardcover, and softcover, notebooks serve students, professionals, and creatives worldwide.
overview.fun_fact: Over 2.4 billion paper notebooks were sold globally in 2023, despite widespread adoption of digital devices — proving the humble notebook remains an enduring everyday essential.

raw_materials.name: Wood Pulp (Virgin Cellulose Fiber)
raw_materials.notes: Approximately 2.2 kg of wood is needed to produce 1 kg of paper from virgin fibers. Major sourcing from boreal forests and tropical plantations.

raw_materials.name: Recycled Paper Fiber
raw_materials.notes: Around 40% of global notebooks used recycled paper content in 2023, with share expected to rise to ~50% by 2025 driven by regulation and consumer demand.

raw_materials.name: Cardboard / Paperboard (Cover)
raw_materials.notes: Used for softcover and hardcover notebook covers. Increasingly sourced as FSC-certified or recycled board.

raw_materials.name: Metal Wire / Steel Spiral (Binding)
raw_materials.notes: Used in spiral- and Wire-O-bound notebooks, which account for over 60% of global notebook sales by volume.

raw_materials.name: Adhesive Glue (PVA / Hot Melt)
raw_materials.notes: Used in perfect-bound and case-bound notebooks. Eco-conscious manufacturers substitute with water-based or non-toxic adhesives.

raw_materials.name: Printing Ink (Petroleum-based or Soy/Water-based)
raw_materials.notes: Used to print ruling lines, covers, and branding. Soy-based and water-based inks are preferred eco-alternatives to petroleum-based inks.

raw_materials.name: Processing Chemicals (Bleaching Agents, Sizing)
raw_materials.notes: Chlorine-free (TCF) and elemental-chlorine-free (ECF) bleaching are increasingly adopted to reduce dioxin discharge into waterways.

manufacturing.title: Raw Material Preparation
manufacturing.description: Logs are debarked and mechanically chipped into small fragments. Chips are screened to remove fines and oversized pieces before being fed into the pulping process. Wood species selection (e.g., eucalyptus, pine, spruce) affects final paper quality.

manufacturing.title: Pulping & Bleaching
manufacturing.description: Wood chips are chemically or mechanically broken down into a soft cellulose slurry. The pulp is then bleached — ideally using chlorine-free methods (TCF/ECF) — to achieve the desired whiteness. This step consumes the most water and energy, with a ~99:1 ratio of water to wood pulp.

manufacturing.title: Sheet Formation & Calendering
manufacturing.description: Bleached pulp is diluted and spread in thin layers onto a moving wire mesh, where water drains away and the fibers bond. The wet sheet is pressed and dried. Calendering rolls then smooth and compress the paper to the required thickness (typically 52–100 g/m² for notebooks).

manufacturing.title: Ruling & Printing
manufacturing.description: Large rolls of paper (parent rolls) are fed through roller-based printing presses that apply ink to create ruled lines, grids, or dot patterns. Cover materials are also printed with branding, designs, and logos using screen, digital, or offset printing. Ink is dried to prevent smudging.
by_products: Volatile organic compounds (VOCs) from solvent inks

manufacturing.title: Cutting & Collating
manufacturing.description: Printed paper rolls are cut to the required notebook size (e.g., A4, A5, B5) using precision guillotine or rotary cutting machines. Individual sheets are collated into page bundles of the correct count for each notebook SKU.

manufacturing.title: Binding
manufacturing.description: Page bundles are bound using one of three primary methods: (1) Spiral/Wire-O — metal coils threaded through punched holes; (2) Perfect/Glue binding — pages glued at the spine using PVA or hot-melt adhesive; (3) Sewn/Thread binding — signatures stitched together for premium durability.

manufacturing.title: Cover Attachment & Finishing
manufacturing.description: Covers made from cardboard, leatherette, or plastic are attached to the bound page block. Finishing operations include corner rounding, foil stamping, embossing, lamination, and elastic band or ribbon attachment for premium products.

manufacturing.title: Quality Inspection
manufacturing.description: Finished notebooks undergo quality checks for ink adhesion, paper thickness, binding strength, cover alignment, and page count accuracy. Defective units are rejected and materials recovered where possible.

manufacturing.title: Packaging & Distribution
manufacturing.description: Approved notebooks are wrapped in protective film, stacked into cartons, labeled with barcodes, and palletized for shipping. Products are distributed to wholesalers, retailers, and online marketplaces globally.

certifications: PEFC (Programme for the Endorsement of Forest Certification)
key_concerns: Deforestation and loss of biodiversity from virgin wood pulp sourcing
High water consumption — producing 1 kg of paper requires at least 324 liters of water
Water pollution from chlorine bleaching releasing dioxins and toxins into waterways
Energy-intensive pulping and papermaking contributing ~1.0–1.5 kg CO2 per kg of paper
Plastic binding components (spiral wire, laminate covers) hinder full recyclability
Petroleum-based inks releasing volatile organic compounds (VOCs)
Short fiber lifespan — paper can only be recycled 5–7 times before fibers degrade
Supply chain carbon from long-distance transport of pulp and finished goods
